truthAudit.Summary: Truth audit of Run 2 findings against 20 true vulnerabilities. Run 2 identified 12 of 20 vulnerabilities (60% detection rate). 8 vulnerabilities were completely missed. Several findings had severity or CWE misclassifications. All 12 cited findings are supported by source evidence with accurate line quotes.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Format String Vulnerability in Debug Logging | CWE-134 | severity: High | Line 237: `std::string active_format = runtime_log_format();` Line 238: `printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input);`
Items.Notes: Run 2 found the format-string injection via runtime_log_format() and printf(). The vulnerability was correctly identified but severity was rated High instead of Critical.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Not present in Run 2 findings
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify the integer overflow in factorial computation (result *= i in a loop without overflow checking).

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Use-After-Free in Deferred History Flush | CWE-416 | severity: High | Lines 817: `char* scratch = static_cast<char*>(memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(copy_len + 1));` captured by lambda at line 821. In cleanup() at lines 883-884: `stage_deferred_history_flush(); memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup();`
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the use-after-free where scratch memory allocated from pool is captured in lambda, then pool is cleaned up before lambda executes. Severity was rated High instead of Critical.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Command Injection in Config Validator | CWE-78 | severity: High | Line 564: `std::string validator_cmd = get_setting("validator_path", "/usr/bin/validator");` Line 566: `std::string command = validator_cmd + " " + config_file;` Line 567: `int result = system(command.c_str());`
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified command injection via config validation. Severity was rated High instead of Critical.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Stack Buffer Overflow in Expression Evaluation | CWE-120 | severity: High | Line 831: `if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) {` Line 832: `strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str());`
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the stack buffer overflow in evaluate_expression where strcpy is used without bounds checking when expression length >= buffer size.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Race Condition in ThreadSafeCounter | CWE-362 | severity: Medium | Lines 483-485: `long long old_value = value_; std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_); value_ = old_value + 1;`
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the race condition where value_ is read outside the lock before being written inside it.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Not present in Run 2 findings
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify the logic bomb in authentication where EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE bypasses authentication after 5 failed attempts.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Path Traversal in Config Loader | CWE-22 | severity: Medium | Line 543: `std::string full_path = config_dir_ + filename;`
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified path traversal via unsanitized filename concatenation.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Heap Buffer Overflow in Expression Parser | CWE-120 | severity: High | Line 367: `expression_buffer_ = static_cast<char*>(memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(buffer_size_));` (buffer_size_ = 256) and Line 376: `if (expr_str.length() > 0) strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str());`
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the heap buffer overflow. However, CWE was listed as CWE-120 instead of CWE-122, and severity was rated High instead of Critical.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Hardcoded Administrative Secret | CWE-798 | severity: Medium | Line 73: `constexpr char ADMIN_SECRET[] = "SC_ENT_2025_AUTH";`
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified hardcoded credentials. Severity was rated Medium instead of High.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Weak Session Token Generation | CWE-338 | severity: Medium | Lines 636-639: `std::random_device rd; std::mt19937 gen(rd()); std::uniform_int_distribution<> dis(1000, 9999);`
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified weak PRNG for session tokens. Severity was rated Medium instead of High.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Not present in Run 2 findings
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify the thread-unsafe static localtime cache in log_debug_message.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Not present in Run 2 findings
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify insecure temporary file handling (CWE-377) in append_override_audit_event. While Run 2 found command injection in this function, it did not flag the insecure temp file usage pattern.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Not present in Run 2 findings
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify persistent authentication state (CWE-613) where authenticated_, login_attempts_, and session_token_ persist across the session.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Not present in Run 2 findings
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify unhandled exception in std::stod(tokens[3]) in handle_variable_command.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Not present in Run 2 findings
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify integer underflow in MemoryPool::allocate when splitting blocks (previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES).

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Not present in Run 2 findings
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify unbounded recursion in parse_expression -> parse_term -> parse_factor -> parse_expression with no depth limit.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Not present in Run 2 findings
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify data race in result_cache_ accessed without cache_mutex_ protection in evaluate_cached and evaluate functions.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Not present in Run 2 findings
Items.Notes: Run 2 did not identify TOCTOU race in load_config between std::filesystem::exists() check and std::ifstream file open.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Integer Division by Zero in Route Scoring | CWE-369 | severity: Low | Line 751: `size_t token_length = (token_end == std::string::npos) ? input.length() : token_end;` Line 752: `return 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length);`
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified integer division by zero when token_length is 0.

Items.Key: command injection in admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|602|609|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|line 605-608: `if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); system(cmd.c_str()); }`|authenticated admin users can execute arbitrary system commands, leading to full system compromise.|admin user sends command starting with 'exec ' followed by arbitrary shell command|replace system() with a whitelist-based command executor or remove the exec functionality entirely.->command injection in admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|602|609|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|line 605: `if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") {` line 606: `std::string cmd = command.substr(5);` line 608: `system(cmd.c_str());`|authenticated admin users can execute arbitrary system commands, leading to full system compromise.|admin user sends command starting with 'exec ' followed by arbitrary shell command|replace system() with a whitelist-based command executor or remove the exec functionality entirely.

command injection in audit event logging|command injection|cwe-78|medium|0.85|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|633|admin::adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|line 626 and 630-632: `std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path;` and `system(command.c_str());`|if tmp/temp environment variables are attacker-controllable, arbitrary commands could be injected.|attacker-controlled temp or tmpdir environment variable|use c++ file i/o (std::ofstream) instead of shell commands for file operations.->command injection in audit event logging|command injection|cwe-78|medium|0.85|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|633|admin::adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|line 626: `std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path;` line 630: `std::string command = "sh -c \"echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path + \"\"";` line 632: `system(command.c_str());`|if tmp/temp environment variables are attacker-controllable, arbitrary commands could be injected.|attacker-controlled temp or tmpdir environment variable|use c++ file i/o (std::ofstream) instead of shell commands for file operations.
